summary: The City Council approved Providence's annual budget that will determine how much money gets spent on city services like schools, police, and road repairs. They also moved forward with a new law aimed at reforming how the city handles young people who get in trouble with the law.

title: Providence City Council Passes FY26 City Budget with Fairer Tax Rates for Working Families

summary: The Providence City Council approved the budget for the upcoming fiscal year (FY26), which includes changes to tax rates designed to be more fair to working families. This means the city has decided how it will spend money and collect taxes in a way that aims to reduce the burden on regular workers and families.

This historic change will strengthen independent oversight of the Providence Police Department and increase accountability.

summary: The City Council President has scheduled a vote to try to override the mayor's rejection of a rent control law that would limit how much landlords can raise rents. If the override vote succeeds, the rent stabilization rules would become law even though the mayor vetoed it.

title: Providence Files First Enforcement Action Under Rent-Setting Algorithm Ban at Direction of the City Council

summary: The City of Providence has taken its first legal action against a landlord for illegally using rent-setting software that artificially drives up rental prices. This enforcement follows a ban passed by the City Council to stop landlords from using computer algorithms to coordinate rent increases across properties.

title: Providence City Council Passes Rent Stabilization

summary: The Providence City Council passed a new law to limit how much landlords can raise rent each year, aiming to help keep housing affordable for residents. The rent control measure is meant to protect tenants from big, sudden rent increases that could force them out of their homes.

summary: The Providence City Council passed a rent stabilization measure for the first time, which would limit how much landlords can increase rent each year to help keep housing affordable. This is a significant step toward protecting renters from large, sudden rent increases that can force them to move.

summary: A new poll found that most Providence residents strongly support rent stabilization policies to help keep housing costs affordable. The survey shows widespread concern about rising rents and support for measures that would limit how much landlords can increase rent each year.
